evidence.description:
Tested USER.md merge logic with two test cases:

Test Case 1 (partial overlap): Created a target USER.md with entries 'Name: Test User' and 'Role: Developer' plus unique entries 'Company: Acme Corp' and 'Position: Senior Engineer'. Source USER.md had overlapping entries plus unique entries '## Preferences', '- Editor: VS Code', '- OS: macOS'. After migration WITHOUT --overwrite, the target USER.md correctly preserved all original entries and appended only the new unique entries from source. No duplicate lines were found.

Test Case 2 (full overlap): Created a target USER.md identical to the source. Migration correctly reported 'skipped' with reason 'All user entries already present' and the target USER.md was unchanged.

commands.command: HOME=$FAKE_HOME_2 npx tsx scripts/migrate-to-nanocore.ts --source openclaw --execute --target-workspace $TEST_TARGET_MEM --target-env $TEST_TARGET_MEM/.env --output-dir $TEST_OUTPUT_MEM (Test Case 1: partial overlap)
commands.output:
Migration Report: openclaw
Mode: execute
Migrated: 10, Archived: 2, Skipped: 6, Conflicts: 0, Errors: 0

Target USER.md after migration contained original entries + merged comment + only new unique entries from source.

commands.command: HOME=$FAKE_HOME_2 npx tsx scripts/migrate-to-nanocore.ts --source openclaw --execute --target-workspace $TEST_TARGET_MEM2 --target-env $TEST_TARGET_MEM2/.env --output-dir $TEST_OUTPUT_MEM2 (Test Case 2: full overlap)
commands.output:
Migration Report: openclaw
Mode: execute
Migrated: 8, Archived: 2, Skipped: 8, Conflicts: 0, Errors: 0

commands.command: python3 -c "import json; data=json.load(open('report.json')); [print(json.dumps(i,indent=2)) for i in data['items'] if i['id']=='user']"
